title: Trust Compression

content.question: What are the top 3 doubts your customer has before joining your business, and how do you resolve them in under 60 seconds?
content.equation: TCD = (Proof Density × Credibility × Early Visibility) ÷ Friction
sop: List the top 3 doubts customers have before buying, pull them from support tickets, sales calls, and DMs.
For each doubt, find or create one piece of proof, a review, data point, or credential, that kills it in under 10 seconds.
Move your strongest proof above the fold or before the first ask, not buried in an FAQ.
Rate Proof Density, Credibility, and Early Visibility 1-10 each, and rate Friction 1-10 (see calculation, page 6)
Remove or simplify one friction point (extra form field, unclear pricing, slow load) each week until trust stops improving.
content.explainer: Every doubt is a friction point. Compress trust by front-loading proof before the ask.
questions: What are the top 3 doubts your customer has before joining your business, and how do you resolve them in under 60 seconds?
Where in your funnel does proof show up too late to matter?
If you removed your weakest piece of friction this week, which doubt would resurface loudest?

definitions.def: The part of a webpage visible before you scroll down. Putting proof above the fold means buyers see it immediately, not buried lower on the page.

content.equation_example: Rate Proof Density, Credibility, and Early Visibility 1-10 each, then rate Friction 1-10. Proof Density 7, Credibility 8, Early Visibility 6, Friction 4 → TCD = (7×8×6)/4 = 84. Higher TCD means doubt gets resolved almost instantly; lower means friction is smothering your proof before it can build trust.
content.closing_statement: Doubt resolved early is trust earned instantly.

caption:
Principle Nº 7: Trust Compression

Trust is the real bottleneck.

Ask yourself: What are the top 3 doubts your customer has before joining your business, and how do you resolve them in under 60 seconds?
